---
title: "Hamiltonians and notation"
---

This page is the single normative statement of every Hamiltonian the
simulator implements. Each formula names the code that realizes it, so the
page can be checked against the source.

## Notation and conventions

Everything is in angular-frequency units with $\hbar = 1$: energies and
drive amplitudes in rad/s, time in seconds, distances in µm.

| Symbol | Meaning | Code |
|---|---|---|
| $\lvert\ell\rangle$ | single-atom level with label $\ell$ (e.g. $\lvert 0\rangle, \lvert 1\rangle, \lvert e\rangle, \lvert r\rangle$) | `LevelStructureSpec.levels` |
| $n^{\ell}_i = \lvert\ell\rangle\langle\ell\rvert_i$ | projector onto level $\ell$ at site $i$ | blocks `n_<level>_<i>`, `sum_n_<level>` |
| $\sigma^x_i = \lvert r\rangle\langle 1\rvert_i + \lvert 1\rangle\langle r\rvert_i$ | two-level transition operator | block `global_X` + h.c. |
| $\Omega$ | Rabi frequency (rad/s); protocols emit the coefficient $\Omega/2$ | `get_drive_coefficients` |
| $\Delta$ | global detuning; sign convention: $H \supset -\Delta\, n^r$ | channel `global_n` / `delta_R` |
| $\delta_i$ | per-site detuning (addressing pin) | `pin_deltas`, channels `global_n_i` |
| $V_{ij} = C_6 / \lvert \mathbf{r}_i - \mathbf{r}_j \rvert^6$ | van der Waals pair interaction | `core.physical_models.vdw_couplings` |
| $\gamma$ | population decay rate; enters as $-\tfrac{i}{2}\gamma$ on the diagonal (non-Hermitian norm loss) | `physical_models` decay flags |
| $A(t)$ | dimensionless Blackman flat-top envelope $\in [0,1]$ | `physics.blackman_pulse` |
| $\varphi(t)$ | 420-nm optical phase; drives enter as $e^{-i\varphi(t)}$ | `phase_420` of the gate protocols |

**Default $C_6$:** $C_6 = 2\pi \times 874\,\text{GHz}\,\mu\text{m}^6$
(`core.level_structures.DEFAULT_C6`); the `lukin` parameter set uses
$C_6 = 2\pi \times 450\,\text{MHz} \times (3\,\mu\text{m})^6$.

## How a Hamiltonian is assembled

`ryd_gate.ir.compile_hamiltonian_ir` builds every backend's input from the
same pieces (`src/ryd_gate/ir.py`):

$$
H(t) \;=\;
\underbrace{H_{\text{const}} + H_{1013} + H_{\text{vdW}}}_{\text{static blocks}}
\;+\; \sum_{c\,\in\,\text{drive channels}} \Big[\, s\, c_c(t)\, B_c \;+\; \text{h.c.}\Big]
\;-\; \sum_i \delta^{\text{pin}}_i n^{r}_i
\;-\; \frac{i}{2} \sum_i \gamma^{\text{sc}}_i\, n^{g}_i ,
$$

where $c_c(t)$ is the protocol's drive coefficient for channel $c$, $B_c$
the registered operator block, and $s$ the `amplitude_scale` of the system
(noise sweeps; the `lightshift_zero` channel scales as $s^2$). The h.c.
term is added only for non-Hermitian transition blocks. The last two sums
are the optional static overlays (`pin_deltas`, `scatter_rates`).

The van der Waals term is always

$$
H_{\text{vdW}} \;=\; \sum_{i<j} V_{ij}\; n^{R}_i\, n^{R}_j ,
\qquad
n^{R}_i \;=\; \sum_{\ell \,\in\, \text{rydberg\_levels}} n^{\ell}_i ,
$$

with the pair list built by `vdw_couplings` over the register coordinates
(`RydbergSystem.set_atom_geom` → block `H_vdw`).

## Two-level lattice (`1r`) — the TFIM workhorse

Levels $(\lvert 1\rangle, \lvert r\rangle)$. With the channel conventions
`global_X` $\to \Omega(t)/2$ on $\sum_i \lvert r\rangle\langle 1\rvert_i$ (+ h.c.)
and `global_n` $\to -\Delta(t)$ on $\sum_i n^r_i$
(`protocols/sweep.py::get_drive_coefficients`):

$$
H(t) \;=\; \frac{\Omega(t)}{2} \sum_i \sigma^x_i
\;-\; \sum_i \big[\Delta(t) + \delta_i(t)\big]\, n^{r}_i
\;+\; \sum_{i<j} V_{ij}\, n^{r}_i n^{r}_j .
$$

This is exactly the Hamiltonian the TN model builder constructs
(`backends/tenpy_mps/model.py`), with $\lvert 1\rangle = \lvert\downarrow\rangle$,
$\lvert r\rangle = \lvert\uparrow\rangle$, $n = (1+\sigma^z)/2$.
`SweepProtocol` supplies $\Omega(t)$, $\Delta(t)$, and per-site
$\delta_i(t)$ as arbitrary functions of time.

### TFIM mapping

Substituting $n = (1+\sigma^z)/2$ gives the transverse-field Ising form
(`protocols/lattice_dynamics.py`, module docstring is normative):

$$
H \;=\; h_x \sum_i \sigma^x_i \;+\; \sum_i h_{z,i}\, \sigma^z_i
\;+\; \sum_{i<j} J_{ij}\, \sigma^z_i \sigma^z_j \;+\; \text{const},
$$

$$
J_{ij} = \frac{V_{ij}}{4}, \qquad
h_x = \frac{\Omega}{2}, \qquad
h_{z,i} = -\frac{\Delta_i}{2} + \frac{1}{4}\sum_j V_{ij} .
$$

`tfim_to_rydberg_controls(hx, hz)` inverts this: $\Omega = 2 h_x$ and
$\Delta_i = 2\big(\tfrac{1}{4}\textstyle\sum_j V_{ij} - h_{z,i}\big)$,
split into a global $\Delta = \langle \Delta_i \rangle$ plus per-site pins
$\delta_i = \Delta_i - \Delta$ that cancel the open-boundary coordination
shifts. `TFIMQuenchProtocol(hx, hz, t_gate)` holds these fields constant;
`TFIMAnnealProtocol` makes $h_x(t), h_z(t)$ time-dependent.

## Three-level lattice (`01r`) — digital-analog

Levels $(\lvert 0\rangle, \lvert 1\rangle, \lvert r\rangle)$; channels
`drive_R` $\to \Omega_R/2$ on $\lvert r\rangle\langle 1\rvert$,
`drive_hf` $\to \Omega_{hf}/2$ on $\lvert 1\rangle\langle 0\rvert$,
`delta_R` $\to -\Delta_R$ on $n^r$, `delta_hf` $\to -\Delta_{hf}$ on $n^1$
(`protocols/digital_analog.py::get_drive_coefficients`):

$$
H(t) = \sum_i \Big[
\frac{\Omega_{R,i}(t)}{2} \lvert r\rangle\langle 1\rvert_i
+ \frac{\Omega_{hf,i}(t)}{2} \lvert 1\rangle\langle 0\rvert_i
+ \text{h.c.}
- \Delta_{R,i}(t)\, n^{r}_i
- \Delta_{hf,i}(t)\, n^{1}_i
\Big]
+ \sum_{i<j} V_{ij}\, n^{r}_i n^{r}_j .
$$

All four profiles may be global or per-site (`DigitalAnalogProtocol`
schedules; per-site channels use the `f"{base}_{i}"` key convention).

## Three-level ladder (`analog_3`)

Levels $(\lvert g\rangle, \lvert e\rangle, \lvert r\rangle)$. The preset
mounts the physical Rb87 picture; single-atom matrices are fixed by
`physical_models._apply_analog_3_lattice_blocks`:

$$
h_{\text{const}} = \big(\Delta - \tfrac{i}{2}\gamma_e\big) \lvert e\rangle\langle e\rvert
- \tfrac{i}{2}\gamma_r \lvert r\rangle\langle r\rvert ,
\qquad
H_{1013} = \frac{\Omega_{1013}}{2} \lvert r\rangle\langle e\rvert + \text{h.c.}
\;\;(\text{static}),
$$

$$
H_{\text{drive}}(t) = c_{420}(t)\, \frac{\Omega_{420}}{2} \lvert e\rangle\langle g\rvert + \text{h.c.},
$$

summed over sites, plus $H_{\text{vdW}}$ on $\lvert r\rangle$. Here
$\Delta$ is the (large) intermediate-state detuning, and the decay rates
$\gamma_e = 1/110.7\,\text{ns}$, $\gamma_r = 1/151.55\,\mu\text{s}$ are
included only when the corresponding `enable_*_decay` flag is set.
$c_{420}(t)$ is the protocol's complex drive coefficient (envelope × phase,
see [Gate drives](#gate-drive-coefficients)).

For a fully *symbolic* three-level ladder (every coefficient — including the
$e \leftrightarrow r$ coupling — chosen by the protocol), hand-build a
`LevelStructureSpec` with the same transitions and pass it to
`RydbergSystem.set_atom_level`; custom specs never mount physical blocks
(Decision D11/D13).

## Seven-level Rb87 (`rb87_7`) — the gate model

Levels $(\lvert 0\rangle, \lvert 1\rangle, \lvert e_1\rangle, \lvert e_2\rangle,
\lvert e_3\rangle, \lvert r\rangle, \lvert r'\rangle)$: the two qubit
hyperfine states, the three $6P_{3/2}$ hyperfine intermediate states
$F \in \{1,2,3\}$, the target Rydberg state, and the "garbage" Rydberg
Zeeman state $\lvert r'\rangle$ reached through polarization leakage.
All matrices are built by `physical_models._apply_rb87_7_lattice_blocks`
with the `our` / `lukin` parameter sets.

**Static single-atom energies** (`_rb87_local_h_const`):

$$
h_{\text{const}} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} \big(\Delta + \epsilon_k - \tfrac{i}{2}\gamma_e\big)
\lvert e_k\rangle\langle e_k\rvert
\;-\; \tfrac{i}{2}\gamma_r \lvert r\rangle\langle r\rvert
\;+\; \big(\delta_Z - \tfrac{i}{2}\gamma_r\big) \lvert r'\rangle\langle r'\rvert ,
$$

with hyperfine offsets
$\epsilon_{1,2,3} = 2\pi \times (-51, 0, +87)\,\text{MHz}$ and the Zeeman
splitting $\delta_Z$ of the garbage Rydberg state.

**420-nm drive** (`_rb87_local_h420`, block `drive_420`; time dependence
comes from the protocol coefficient $c_{420}(t)$):

$$
h_{420} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} \frac{\Omega_{420}\, C_k + \Omega'_{420}\, C'_k}{2}\;
\lvert e_k\rangle\langle 1\rvert ,
$$

where $C_k, C'_k$ are Clebsch–Gordan coefficients of the main and
leakage polarization components and
$\Omega'_{420} = \Omega_{420} \cdot d_{\text{mid}}$ is the
polarization-leakage Rabi frequency (ARC dipole-matrix-element ratio).

**Static 1013-nm coupling** (`_rb87_local_h1013`, blocks `H_1013` +
conjugate):

$$
h_{1013} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} \Big[
\frac{\Omega_{1013}}{2} C_k\, \lvert r\rangle\langle e_k\rvert
+ \frac{\Omega'_{1013}}{2} C'_k\, \lvert r'\rangle\langle e_k\rvert
\Big] + \text{h.c.}
$$

**AC-Stark / scattering of the spectator qubit state**
(`_rb87_local_zero_lightshift`, block `lightshift_zero`, coefficient
$A(t)^2$): the off-resonant 420-nm light shifts $\lvert 0\rangle$ and the
intermediate manifold by second-order couplings
$g_k^2/(E_{e_k} - E_0)$ and adds the elastic-scattering loss
$-\tfrac{i}{2}\sum_k g_k^2 \gamma_e / (E_{e_k}-E_0)^2$ on
$\lvert 0\rangle$, with $E_0 = -2\pi\times 6.835\,\text{GHz}$ the qubit
hyperfine splitting.

**Interaction:** $H_{\text{vdW}}$ with
$n^{R} = \lvert r\rangle\langle r\rvert + \lvert r'\rangle\langle r'\rvert$.

**Parameter sets** (`_rb87_physical_params`):

| quantity | `our` | `lukin` |
|---|---|---|
| Rydberg level | $70S$ | $53S$ |
| $\Delta$ | $2\pi \times 9.1$ GHz | $2\pi \times 7.8$ GHz |
| $\Omega_{420}$ | $2\pi \times 491$ MHz | $2\pi \times 237$ MHz |
| $\Omega_{1013}$ | $2\pi \times 185$ MHz | $2\pi \times 303$ MHz |
| $\Omega_{\text{eff}} = \Omega_{420}\Omega_{1013}/2\lvert\Delta\rvert$ | derived | derived |
| $\gamma_e$ | $1/110.7$ ns | $1/110$ ns |
| $\gamma_r$ | $1/151.55$ µs | $1/88$ µs |

## Gate drive coefficients

The CZ protocols drive only the `drive_420` (+ dagger) and
`lightshift_zero` channels (`protocols/gate_cz.py`):

$$
c_{420}(t) = A(t)\, e^{-i\varphi(t)}, \qquad
c_{\text{ls}}(t) = A(t)^2 ,
$$

with $A(t)$ the Blackman flat-top envelope
(`physics.blackman_pulse(t, t_{\text{rise}}, t_{\text{gate}})`) and the
protocol-specific phase:

| protocol | $\varphi(t)$ |
|---|---|
| `TOProtocol` (time-optimal, 6 params) | $A\cos(\omega t + \varphi_0) + \delta t$ |
| `ARProtocol` (amplitude-robust, 8 params) | $A_1\sin(\omega t + \varphi_1) + A_2\sin(2\omega t + \varphi_2) + \delta t$ |
| `DoubleARPProtocol` | $\dot\varphi(t) = \Delta_{\text{ARP}}(t)$, two super-Gaussian rapid-adiabatic passes; amplitude $A(t) = \Omega_{\text{eff}}(t)/\Omega_{\text{eff}}^{\text{nom}}$, optional dynamic Stark compensation added to the chirp |

The effective two-photon picture behind these pulses is
$\Omega_{\text{eff}} = \Omega_{420}\Omega_{1013} / (2\lvert\Delta\rvert)$
with the time scale $T = 2\pi/\Omega_{\text{eff}}$ used to normalize the
optimization parameters $x$.

## Non-Hermitian decay and Monte Carlo noise

Decay is modeled as norm loss: every enabled rate $\gamma$ enters as
$-\tfrac{i}{2}\gamma$ on the corresponding diagonal, so
$1 - \lVert\psi(t)\rVert^2$ is the accumulated scattering/decay
probability. `analysis.gate_metrics.residuals_to_branching` redistributes
that loss over hyperfine ground states using the ARC branching ratios
(`physics._rydberg_branching_ratios`, `physics._mid_branching_ratios`).
Shot-to-shot noise (`NoiseModel`, `MonteCarloRunner`) samples static
detuning offsets, amplitude scale factors $s$ (entering as $s\,c_{420}$ and
$s^2 c_{\text{ls}}$), local RIN, and atom positions, then averages
observables over shots.
